memoryArtifacts.headline: My Maternal 19th. Great Scottish Grandfather, Roderick Mor MacLeod, 5th. Baron of Lewis
memoryArtifacts.description:
Name: Roderick Mor MacLeod, 5th. Baron of Lewis
Born: about 1320 Scotland
Married: before 1340 in Scotland to Margaret MacDonald
Children: (6) Margaret (Mackintosh), Roderick, Torquil, Tormod, Henault …

zh.lifeSummary: Margaret MacDonald於大約1320年出生在英國蘇格蘭。她於大約1350年與Roderick "Ruaidhri Mor" MacLeod 5th Of Lewis在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1400年在英國蘇格蘭洛-爾易斯爾 本 劉易斯享年82歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭洛-爾易斯爾 本 劉易斯。

nameMeanings.name: MacDonald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McDonald
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Domhnaill ‘son of Domhnall’, a name derived from the Celtic elements domno- ‘world’ + val- ‘might, rule’. Donald is an Anglicized form (via Latin) of this personal name used in Scotland, though the surname is also widespread in Ireland. The name is equivalent to Irish McDonnell and McConnell , and to Manx Cannell .

History: This is the name of the largest and most disparate of the Scottish clans (Clan Donald), associated in particular with the Hebrides and claiming descent from Domhnall mac Raghnaill mac Somhairle, who lived in the late 12th century. From that time until 1493 the head of the clan was known as Lord (or King) of the Isles. The reigns of the Lords of the Isles were always stormy, often in conflict with the kings of Scotland, and peppered with disasters. After a series of defeats in the 1480s, Eoin Mac Dhomhnaill a Ìle (John Macdonald of Islay, Earl of Ross and Lord of the Isles) forfeited his lands, his power, and his title as Lord of the Isles in 1493 to King James IV of Scotland. By then, a branch of the family had settled in the Antrim Glens in Ireland and members moved between the southern Hebrides and Ireland throughout the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret MacDonald was born about 1320, in Scotland, United Kingdom. She married Roderick "Ruaidhri Mor" MacLeod 5th Of Lewis about 1350, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1400, in Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 82, and was buried in Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret MacDonald est née environ 1320, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Roderick "Ruaidhri Mor" MacLeod 5th Of Lewis environ 1350, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1400, à Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterrée à Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Scotland, Uni: Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Schottland, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Margaret MacDonald wurde um 1320 in Schottland, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1350 in Scotland Roderick "Ruaidhri Mor" MacLeod 5th Of Lewis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1400 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret MacDonald(은)는 대략 1320년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1350년 스컷랜드에서 Roderick "Ruaidhri Mor" MacLeod 5th Of Lewis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1400년 영국 스코틀랜드 로스-실르 이슬일 프 루이스에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 로스-실르 이슬일 프 루이스에 묻혔다.

ru.Isle of Lewis, Ross-shire, Scotland, Uni: исл оф львис, росс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Margaret MacDonald родилась около 1320 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roderick "Ruaidhri Mor" MacLeod 5th Of Lewis около 1350 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1400 г. в местности исл оф львис, росс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности исл оф львис, росс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
